section.heading: Expenses incurred in connection with leave canceled due to contingency operations: reimbursement

subsection.heading: Authorization To Reimburse.—
subsection.chapeau: The Secretary concerned may reimburse a member of the armed forces under the jurisdiction of the Secretary for travel and related expenses (to the extent not otherwise reimbursable under law) incurred by the member as a result of the cancellation of previously approved leave when—

paragraph.content: the leave is canceled in connection with the member’s participation in a contingency operation; and
the cancellation occurs within 48 hours of the time the leave would have commenced.

subsection.heading: Regulations.—
subsection.content: The Secretary of Defense and, in the case of the Coast Guard when it is not operating as a service in the Navy, the Secretary of Homeland Security shall prescribe regulations to establish the criteria for the applicability of subsection (a).

subsection.heading: Conclusiveness of Settlement.—
subsection.content: The settlement of an application for reimbursement under subsection (a) is final and conclusive.

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 114–328, div. A, title V, § 522(a), Dec. 23, 2016, 130 Stat. 2115.)
